metric_name: LCDs to outnumber CRTs as standard PC monitor in 2003
metric_value: In 2003, they say, we'll finally see LCDs outnumber CRTs as the standard monitor sold with PCs.

metric_name: LCDs weak on full-motion video; CRTs retain niche
metric_value: Because there's often a latency when it comes to displaying full-motion video on an LCD, games freaks and DVD aficionados are going to quibble with the picture and probably will stick with CRTs for the time being

metric_name: LCD response-time improving with vendor effort
metric_value: There are still issues with watching movies or video clips on the Internet, but response time is getting better and better
By 2006, LCDs will outnumber CRTs by 5 to 1.

metric_name: LCD shipments first exceeded CRT 2003 globally per DisplaySearch
metric_value: 2003 was the first year LCD monitor shipments exceeded CRT monitor shipments per DisplaySearch/iSuppli tracking

metric_name: CRT fully phased out of mainstream PC monitors
metric_value: Major CRT monitor manufacturers exited by 2008; last Sony CRT TV production ended 2008; CRT PC monitors essentially unavailable in retail by 2010
